title: Boat Rental in Lake Maggiore: a Budget Guide for 2026
topic: a budget guide to renting a boat in Lake Maggiore
html:
Boat rental in Lake Maggiore need not break the bank. For as little as €150 you can take the helm of a small motorboat from Arona for a half-day outing. Compare verified options and pricing on BoatHire24 to find a deal that suits your budget.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Lake Maggiore?

Prices start from around €100 for a shared tour to over €800 for a crewed yacht for a full day. The total depends on boat type, season, and duration. Budget-conscious travellers can still enjoy the lake without spending a fortune.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Lake Maggiore?

Most small motorboats up to 40hp can be hired without a licence in Italy. Larger or faster boats require a valid boating licence. Always check with the operator, as exact requirements may vary; the platform has a search filter for licence-free rentals.

Where to set off in Lake Maggiore

Lake Maggiore offers several convenient departure points. Arona, at the southern end, is a lively town with multiple boat hire operators. Stresa, opposite the Borromean Islands, provides easy access to Isola Bella and Isola Madre. Further north, Cannero Riviera and Baveno are quieter but offer equally beautiful routes. For a longer day trip, start from Luino on the eastern shore.

Popular destinations include the rocky Castelli di Cannero, the Palazzo Borromeo on Isola Bella, and the tranquil Hermitage of Santa Caterina del Sasso. The distance from Arona to the Borromean Islands is roughly 12 nautical miles, a comfortable half-day cruise.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4, 6 guests), ideal for a half-day exploring coves; from €150 half-day.
  2. Bowrider (6, 8 guests), great for families, with space for swimming stops; from €250 half-day.
  3. Day cruiser (8, 10 guests), a covered cabin and sundeck for full-day trips; from €400 full-day.
  4. Luxury yacht with crew (10, 12 guests), all-inclusive, captain and host; from €800 full-day.
  5. Rigid inflatable boat (6, 8 guests), fast and nimble for adventure; from €200 half-day.

For a well-equipped example, see the 28-foot Axopar, known for comfortable seating and a 200hp outboard; you can browse realistic prices from the Limpopo charter page.

the Limpopo charter page

You can find similar boats on the platform.

Cost breakdown for Lake Maggiore boat hire

  • Shared guided tour (2, 3 hours): €50, €80 per person.
  • Small motorboat without licence (half-day, fuel included): €150, €250.
  • Catamaran for up to 10 guests (full-day with skipper): €500, €700.
  • Luxury crewed yacht including lunch (full-day): €800, €1,200.

These price bands reflect 2026 rates. For a broader view of available options, browse boat rentals. You can compare verified boat listings and read reviews to ensure you get the best value.

day charters in Lake Maggiore are widely available through the platform.

If you hire a boat from Stresa in high season (July, August), aim to leave the marina before 10am to avoid the afternoon breeze known as the Inverna, which can make mooring trickier at Isola Bella.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €1,200 per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 guests on crewed yachts
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Not required for boats up to 40hp

Questions about boat rental in Lake Maggiore

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Lake Maggiore?

Yes, you can rent small motorboats with engines under 40hp without a licence from operators in Arona and Stresa.

What is the best month for a boat trip on Lake Maggiore?

May and September offer warm weather with fewer crowds, while July and August are the peak season with reliable sun and all services running.

How long does it take to cross the lake from Arona to Stresa?

A direct motorboat crossing takes about 30 minutes, but a leisurely cruise stopping at the Borromean Islands can fill a whole morning.

Are pets allowed on hired boats?

Many operators allow well-behaved dogs, but you must confirm when booking; some charge a small cleaning fee.
